This includes designation as a proscribed terrorist group or organisation according to the UK Home Office, as well as the use of Royal Prerogative Powers or Orders in Council, legislation signed into law, or the creation of a new national designation that formally designates the IRGC as a terrorist organization.

Statements by government officials, including the Prime Minister, Home Office, or National Crime Service, will not count unless accompanied by a formal designation.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting or official information from the UK Government.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 97%, reflecting the UK's Terrorism Act 2000 prohibition on proscribing state-backed entities like Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), necessitating new legislation that Prime Minister Keir Starmer pledged on April 24 for the post-State Opening parliamentary session starting May 13. No bill has been introduced as of mid-May, leaving insufficient time—roughly seven weeks—for Commons and Lords readings, committee scrutiny, amendments, and royal assent before the June 30 deadline, consistent with historical patterns for counter-terrorism reforms. While the EU proscribed the IRGC in February, UK foreign policy prioritizes diplomatic channels with Tehran. Realistic shifts could arise from a major IRGC-linked incident prompting fast-tracked emergency powers, though such escalations remain speculative.

markets.question: Will Cristiano Ronaldo announce his retirement in 2026?
markets.description:
This polymarket will resolve to "Yes" if Cristiano Ronaldo officially announces his retirement from club soccer by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

To resolve "Yes", the announcement must clearly state that Ronaldo is retiring from club-level professional soccer. The announcement must come from Ronaldo himself or his official representatives.

Retirement from international soccer alone will not qualify. 

The retirement must be intended to take effect immediately or prior to the start of the next club season following the announcement.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from Cristiano Ronaldo; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

The 41-year-old Portugal captain, managed for fitness ahead of the 2026 World Cup (confirmed as his final major international tournament last November), has added goals like his 969th career tally in AFC Champions League Two play, chasing 1,000 while showing no injury concerns or performance dips. Prior statements emphasize retiring post-World Cup or upon reaching milestones, with no recent developments signaling an earlier exit.

eventMetadata.context_description: Germany enters the May 31 international friendly in Mainz as overwhelming trader favorites at 85% implied probability, driven by their superior FIFA ranking, home advantage at MEWA Arena, and recent dominant form including Florian Wirtz's masterclass—two goals and two assists—in a thrilling victory over Switzerland six weeks ago. Head-to-head history favors Die Mannschaft, unbeaten against Finland with a 2-0 win in their last 2016 friendly, underscoring the quality gap. Despite Serge Gnabry's long-term muscle tear ruling him out for the 2025-26 season and recovery concerns for Jamal Musiala and Kai Havertz, Germany's squad depth maintains edge. Finland, hampered by Lucas Bergström's injury and lacking firepower, sees draw and win at 10% each, reflecting limited upset potential absent major German absences.

markets.question: Will ByteDance have the highest IPO Market Cap 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the company that achieves the highest market capitalization in U.S. dollars based on the official closing price on its first trading day in 2026.

This market will resolve to a company that completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

Market capitalization is defined as the total number of outstanding shares multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If two or more companies have exactly equal highest closing market capitalizations, this market will resolve to the company whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In case the respective company's primary exchange’s official listing page does not report in U.S. dollars, it will be converted to U.S. dollars using the U.S. Federal Reserve Board’s H.10 foreign exchange reference rate for the relevant currency pair on the company’s first trading day (https://www.federalreserve.gov/releases/h10/). If no such rate is available for the relevant trading day, the most recent previously published rate will be used. If the relevant currency is not listed, another credible exchange rate source will be used.

A listed company may resolve to "No" as soon as it becomes unable to complete an IPO, including due to acquisition, merger, or absorption by an entity that is already publicly traded.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on the respective companies’ first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

Recent disclosures, including dual-class shares granting Elon Musk supermajority voting control, have solidified sentiment amid Starlink profitability funding AI ambitions. xAI lingers at 25.5% on merger uncertainties, while Anthropic's 9.4% reflects its explosive $30 billion revenue run-rate from Claude AI enterprise adoption, though trailing SpaceX's scale; upcoming roadshows and regulatory reviews remain key catalysts before year-end resolutions.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Ankara be 25°C on May 12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Esenboğa Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 12 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Esenboğa Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/tr/%C3%A7ubuk/LTAC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices 22°C at 40% implied probability for Ankara's highest temperature on May 12, reflecting the latest GFS and ECMWF model ensembles projecting peak daytime highs of 22-23°C under a building high-pressure ridge promoting subsidence and solar heating. This follows a warming trend after an early May cold anomaly that suppressed temperatures below 15°C last week, with yesterday's observations nearing 21°C per Turkish State Meteorological Service (MGM) data. Partly cloudy skies with low precipitation risk (under 40% for isolated thunderstorms) limit extremes, consistent with early May climatology averaging 20-22°C at Esenboğa International Airport station, where the market resolves. Overnight model updates could refine odds before tomorrow's observations.

markets.question: Will Seoul have less than 100mm of precipitation in May?

The resolution source for this market will be the Korea Meteorological Administration, specifically the figure for monthly precipitation in mm when the classification is set for "ground", the region/branch is set for "Seoul", the element is set for "precipitation", and the period is set for the month of May at the https://data.kma.go.kr/climate/RankState/selectRankStatisticsDivisionList.do link once the figure is finalized for the whole month of May 2026.

If the relevant data is not available by July 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

The resolution source for this market measures precipitation to 1 decimal place (e.g., 1.5), thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from government of the United States of America, official information from Trump or released by his verified social media accounts (e.g. https://twitter.com/POTUS), however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Chinese Foreign Ministry announced on May 11 that President Donald Trump will pay a state visit to China from May 13 to 15 at President Xi Jinping's invitation, driving trader consensus to price a May 13 arrival at 95% implied probability amid confirmed advance U.S. logistics teams and C-17 flights to Beijing since early May. This formal diplomatic commitment overrides earlier uncertainties around trade tariffs, Taiwan tensions, and the Iran conflict, where Trump seeks Beijing's influence on the Strait of Hormuz. With odds exceeding 90%, realistic challenges include sudden escalation in Iran hostilities, health emergencies, or last-minute cancellations due to bilateral disputes, though no such signals have emerged.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the lead-up to the May 26 Republican primary runoff for Texas U.S. Senate, trader consensus favors Attorney General Ken Paxton at 59.5% implied probability over incumbent Sen. John Cornyn's 40%, reflecting recent polls showing Paxton ahead amid a heated intraparty contest. After neither secured a March 3 primary majority—Paxton drawing strong support from Trump-aligned voters critical of Cornyn's establishment ties—surveys like the University of Houston Hobby School poll (released May 5) indicate Paxton leading 48%-45%, with others like Reps. Beth Van Duyne, Dawn Buckingham, and Wesley Hunt distant also-rans at under 1%. Cornyn maintains a fundraising edge with over $8 million cash-on-hand, while President Trump has teased an endorsement "soon," potentially tipping the closely contested balance before early voting begins.

markets.question: Obras vs. San Lorenzo
markets.description:
In the upcoming LNB game, scheduled for February 12 at 6:30PM ET:
If the Obras win, the market will resolve to "Obras".
If the San Lorenzo win, the market will resolve to "San Lorenzo".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Obras Sanitarias commands 78.5% trader consensus as heavy favorites against San Lorenzo in this Liga Nacional de Básquet matchup, driven by their commanding head-to-head dominance—four straight victories, including a gritty 76-70 home win on April 18. Sitting comfortably in fourth place amid playoff contention, Obras boasts superior recent form with a balanced attack led by Marcos Mata and Juan Brussino, despite a tight 73-75 loss to Instituto last outing. San Lorenzo, mired in 15th near the relegation zone, grapples with defensive woes and subpar away splits, amplifying Obras' home-court edge at Estadio Obras Sanitarias and overall momentum in the tightly contested LNB standings.

markets.question: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 1.5% and 1.7%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in South Korea in the 12-month period ending December 2026, as reported by the South Korean Ministry of Data and Statistics (MODS).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in South Korea’s Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026 (December CPI % change compared to the same month of the previous year), according to the monthly MODS Consumer Price Survey report for the specified month.

The resolution source for this market will be the MODS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on December 31, 2026. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

The relevant report will be made available upon release at: https://mods.go.kr/board.es?mid=a20109020000&bid=11751&eng_board_type=01

Note: This market’s resolution source reports percentage change in the South Korean Consumer Price Index to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.0%). Thus this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. For the full release schedule, see: https://mods.go.kr/schdl.es?mid=a20301000000

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders' closely contested implied probabilities—clustered tightly around the 2.1%–2.9% bins for South Korea's 2026 annual CPI inflation—reflect April 2026's year-over-year headline rate accelerating to 2.6%, a 21-month high driven by surging petroleum prices amid Middle East tensions, while core inflation held steady at 2.2%. This uptick from March's 2.2% has edged sentiment toward the higher 2.7%–2.9% outcome at 33.3%, though Bank of Korea projections of mid-to-high 2% averages, IMF's 2.5% forecast, and stable underlying pressures keep lower bins competitive at 29.9%–32.1%. Key swing factors include May CPI data and the BOK's late-May policy meeting, where persistent energy costs could prompt a hawkish pivot from the 2.50% base rate.

markets.question: Will R.C. Landingham win Bareback Riding at The American Rodeo 2026 Championship Weekend?
markets.description:
This market will resolve in favor of the rider who is named the winner of Bareback Riding at the 2026 The American Rodeo Championship Weekend 2026, per the rules of American Rodeo. Otherwise, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If a rider is disqualified or otherwise ineligible to participate in the listed event per the rules of American Rodeo, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 The American Rodeo Championship Weekend 2026 are canceled, postponed or no winner has been declared by May 23, 2026, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from American Rodeo; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the 2026 American Rodeo semifinals roster finalized after the West Regional Finals concluded May 2 in Las Vegas, trader consensus centers on top PRCA-ranked invitees and regional standouts in bareback riding, where elite spur riders like Keenan Hayes lead sentiment due to consistent 85+ point rides on tough broncs this season and prior podium finishes at Globe Life Field. East Regional winner Tilden Hooper, Central qualifiers Ty Pope and Leighton Berry, plus West advancers including Richmond Champion and Chett Deitz, earned spots via aggregate scores, bolstering a competitive 20-rider field for the May 22 semifinals. Recent PRCA momentum favors high-output veterans like Rocker Steiner and Wacey Schalla, while random draws and bucking horse matchups loom large ahead of the May 23 championship round for the $1 million bonus.

markets.question: Will Laurent Blanc be appointed as manager of Manchester United?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the person who is appointed as the next permanent manager of Manchester United.

If no permanent manager is appointed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other." 

Appointments of 'interim,' 'caretaker,' or other non-permanent managers will not impact this market's resolution.

An announcement of a new permanent manager's appointment before this market's close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option, regardless of when the announced appointment goes into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Manchester United; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Michael Carrick's 86% implied probability as next permanent Manchester United head coach stems from his transformative interim tenure since Ruben Amorim's January sacking, with United amassing the Premier League's most points under him and nearing Champions League qualification. Club insiders report full INEOS alignment, dressing-room backing, and an imminent formal offer, bolstered by Carrick's tactical acumen, player relationships, and deep Old Trafford knowledge—discarding rivals like Thomas Tuchel or Gareth Southgate. While dominant, scenarios like season-ending flops, Carrick declining for family reasons, or a high-profile candidate emerging post-transfer window could shift trader consensus.

markets.question: Will the Starship for SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12 achieve a successful splashdown?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Starship upper stage for the twelfth Starship-SuperHeavy test flight achieves a controlled splashdown in the ocean. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market pertains to the twelfth Starship launch. If the rocket lifts off the pad, it will be considered the twelfth Starship launch, and this market will resolve based on it. If the rocket does not lift off the pad, it will not be considered the twelfth launch, and this market will stay open until the twelfth launch has occurred. If the twelfth launch has not occurred by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

A splashdown will be considered controlled if the Starship upper stage successfully re-enters Earth's atmosphere, remains intact during descent, and enters the water without breaking apart or exploding upon impact.

The resolution source for this market will be official video provided by SpaceX (https://www.youtube.com/c/SpaceX), as well as secondary video feeds and/or written reports if necessary.

events.title: SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12
events.description: This is a market on the outcome of the SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12.

eventMetadata.context_description: SpaceX's Starship Flight Test 12 represents the inaugural flight of the upgraded Version 3 Super Heavy booster—featuring 33 Raptor engines—and Starship upper stage, achieving full stack integration at Starbase's Pad 2 on May 9 after Booster 19's successful full-duration static fire on May 7. These rapid milestones, following Elon Musk's early April timeline of 4-6 weeks, underscore iterative progress in reusable rocket technology amid FAA licensing approval. Trader consensus hinges on SpaceX's testing cadence outpacing competitors like Blue Origin, with sentiment buoyed by hardware reliability gains. Watch for launch rehearsals and the NET May 12-15 window, where weather or minor anomalies could influence resolution.
